---
title: "Truss Analysis"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
canvas:
  type: assignment
  published: true
  points: 20
  grading_type: points
  submission_types: [online_upload]
  allowed_extensions: [pdf, py, ipynb]
  unlock_at: "2026-09-02T08:00:00Z"
  due_at: "2026-09-16T23:59:00Z"
  lock_at: "2026-09-23T23:59:00Z"
---

# Truss Analysis

Analyse the statically determinate truss below using the **method of joints**.

## Governing Equations

At every pin joint the forces are in equilibrium:

$$
\sum F_x = 0, \qquad \sum F_y = 0.
$$

A member of length $L$, area $A$, and modulus $E$ carrying axial force $N$
elongates by

$$
\delta = \frac{N L}{A E}.
$$

## Expected Member Forces (sample)

| Member | Length (m) | Force (kN) | State |
|:-------|-----------:|-----------:|:------|
| AB | 3.0 | +12.5 | Tension |
| BC | 4.0 | −8.0 | Compression |
| AC | 5.0 | +6.4 | Tension |

## Submission

Upload a single `.pdf`, `.py`, or `.ipynb` showing your free-body diagrams and
calculations. Due dates and a one-week late window are configured on Canvas.
